description: §19.3.3 E-ERROR-009 — `fail` target is not an error-enum variant (a bare string). `fail` must name a variant of the declared error type `MyError`; a non-enum target fires E-ERROR-009 AS an error. Incidental W-DEAD-FUNCTION/W-PROGRAM-001 ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §19.3.3: the variant specified in a `fail` statement SHALL be a valid variant of the declared error enum type; a `fail` target that is not an enum variant SHALL be a compile error (E-ERROR-009).
